content.full_text:
Fair Political Practices Commission  
Attn: John Wallace  
February 24, 1992

in fact determines that the current environmental laws are inadequate to protect the Delta and that such protection is important enough to override local control.

One result would be that the City of Lathrop would be unable to urbanize any of the Delta within the General Plan boundary for the City of Lathrop.

Another result may be that the City of Lathrop would be allowed to develop to the limits of the General Plan, since the General Plan has been adopted prior to any action to limit development.

A third result may be that there will be some control over development in the Delta and that the City of Lathrop and other cities would have to comply with the additional State control over development.

Any State action which would limit or restrict the City of Lathrop's development of land within the Delta area would necessarily also restrict and limit Councilmember McKee's development of his own property within the Delta area.

The issue is whether or not Councilmember McKee's participation as a councilperson on a committee is a conflict of interest and a violation of the Fair Political Practices Act, in view of his ownership interest of real property within the Delta area.

Councilmember McKee's participation on the committee, which is composed of the mayor and one councilmember, would be to meet with and lobby with various governmental persons and agencies as to the Delta issues and their effect on the City of Lathrop's development within the City of Lathrop's General Plan.

Your assistance would be greatly appreciated.

Very truly yours,

Law Offices of KROLL LOEFFLER & WAGGONER

DAVID CERVANTES

DC/mer
